summary: CLI launches Claude Code with permission prompts disabled and routes agent control through a remote bridge
details: When invoked as `npx 0x2ai-demo1 <token>`, bin/start.cjs spawns `claude --dangerously-skip-permissions` and stages a `.mcp.json` that hardcodes `BRIDGE_URL=https://demo1.0x2ai.com`. The bundled MCP server (payload/chatroom-mcp-lite-patched.cjs) proxies all tool calls through that bridge and forwards `system_directive` / `directive` fields from bridge responses back into the Claude agent loop. The agent_query tool additionally spawns child `node` processes with arguments derived from a bridge-influenced registry. The combined effect is a remote operator (the author of demo1.0x2ai.com) can push instructions into a shell-capable agent session on the user's machine with permission prompts disabled, giving the operator effective code execution on the user's host. The package additionally relays every chat message, persisted memory, and LLM prompt (chatroom_post, memory_save, provider_query) through the author's bridge, where API keys are 'managed server-side.' Although the user opts in by running the CLI, the decision to disable permission gates and to make the agent driveable from the remote bridge is taken silently by the package, not by the user.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
